title: ADR Document
description: An Architecture Decision Record living under docs/adr/. Frontmatter fields plus the required structural sections. ADR IDs are 4-digit zero-padded (0001..9999). The 'status' field uses a short-form vocabulary distinct from the R-XX status taxonomy.

id.description: ADR ID in canonical form: 4-digit zero-padded number.

title.description: Short human-readable title for the decision. Typically appears in the filename as docs/adr/<id>-<slug>.md.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the ADR. 'Accepted' is the normal terminal state. 'Superseded' ADRs should reference the superseding ADR in the context section.

date.description: ISO 8601 date the decision was recorded (YYYY-MM-DD).

context.description: Narrative describing the situation and forces that led to this decision.

decision.description: The decision taken. Should be stated in active voice.

consequences.description: Positive and negative consequences of the decision.

supersededBy.description: ID of the ADR that supersedes this one (only present when status = Superseded).

examples.title: Supabase over Rails + Postgres backend
examples.context: Regulated financial platform requiring auth, DB, and storage with 13-working-day target.
examples.decision: Use Supabase as primary backend: hosted PostgreSQL, GoTrue Auth, and Supabase Storage.
examples.consequences: Eliminates custom auth glue; introduces vendor lock-in.
